rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY** CYNTHIA CHRISTOPHER ET AL VS. INVITATION HOMES REALTY CALIFORNIA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 3, 2021 line 1. Motion For Change Of Venue Hearing Required to address Buran Equipment Co. v. Superior Court, 190 Cal. App. 3d 1662. Counsel for Plaintiff and moving defendants to be present. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARIA DE LA CRUZ ET AL VS. CLARA BELLE HAYNES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 3, 2021 line 3. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash is GRANTED. Purported sub-service at the UPS store on the first attempt is improper in light of Plaintiff's actual knowledge of other service address at the time of the service attempt. Wobbe Declaration, paragraph 4. Therefore, November 19, 2020 service is quashed. The Court notes that this issue may be moot in light of January 2020 service (POS filed on March 11, 2021) not addressed in this motion filed on March 3, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JUNE LEE VS. 460 FRANCISCO ST H.O.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 3, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT 460 FRANCISCO ST. H.O.A.'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to (1) clearly indicate which cause of action is alleged against which defendant; (2) allege facts in support of each element of the cause of action for breach of contract, i.e. did the HOA fail to maintain/or repair/or insure the common areas; how did this failure cause damage to the plaintiff; what is the damage; (3) allege facts in support of each element of causes of action for negligent and intentional infliction of emotional distress. Demurrer to the cause of action for defamation is moot. Plaintiff concedes that this cause of action is not asserted against the moving defendant. Amended complaint shall so clearly indicate. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
